ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Ham Chim Peng; mapped to the closest parent class 'fried dough food product' (FOODON_03315595) as an approximate match. A species-level entry for this Singaporean Cantonese snack does not exist in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Doughnuts and similar fried dough products) is the closest defensible category for Ham Chim Peng. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for this specific Singaporean Cantonese deep-fried dough fritter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Ham Chim Peng or equivalent Cantonese fried dough fritter as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Ham Chim Peng weighs approximately 80 g, which is the typical serving size sold at hawker centres and morning markets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Ham Chim Peng — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Ham Chim Peng (CR ID: F11090020). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this exact match. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for fried dough (SR Legacy #18033) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Ham Chim Peng in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated from the closest analogous food: white wheat bread / refined white-flour deep-fried dough products (GI ~70–85, mean ~76) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Deep-frying of refined wheat dough without significant fibre produces a very high GI consistent with this range. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 43.0) / 100 = 32.7 ≈ 32.8, using 80 g serving size (43.0 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 390 mg/100 g with negligible potassium offset and deep-fried saturated fat content
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Ham Chim Peng to occasional small portions (≤½ piece) due to its sodium content of 390 mg/100 g, absence of meaningful potassium or fibre to offset sodium load, and the cardiovascular compounding effect of deep-fried saturated fat; the AHA recommends limiting sodium to 1500–2300 mg/day and avoiding high-sodium fried foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (53.8 g/100 g), low dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g), estimated GI ~76, and high glycemic load (~32.8 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ham Chim Peng is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high glycemic load (~32.8 per 80 g serving), driven by 53.8 g refined carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.6 g dietary fibre, and an estimated GI of 76; if consumed, limit to a maximum of half a piece and pair with a protein or fat source to partially attenuate the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation with saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g estimated), sodium 390 mg/100 g, and high refined carbohydrate promoting dyslipidaemia
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ham Chim Peng poses a high cardiovascular concern due to its deep-fried preparation contributing saturated fat and potential trans fat residues from frying oil, combined with 390 mg sodium/100 g and a high refined carbohydrate load that promotes postprandial triglyceride elevation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generating advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) and high refined carbohydrate promoting systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Ham Chim Peng is contraindicated for individuals with active rheumatoid arthritis due to deep-frying generating pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), potential trace trans fat from frying oil, and a high refined carbohydrate load (53.8 g/100 g) that drives inflammatory mediators; avoidance is recommended during active flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ham Chim Peng (咸煎饼 / 鹹煎餅; also romanized as Haam Zim Beng in Cantonese) is a traditional Cantonese deep-fried dough fritter that is a beloved street food and hawker snack in Singapore, Malaysia, and among overseas Chinese communities. The name translates literally as 'salty fried biscuit' or 'salty fried cake' in Cantonese. The dough is made from wheat flour leavened with baking powder and alkaline water (lye water), which gives the fritter its characteristic chewy interior and slightly crisp, golden-brown exterior. The dough is shaped into flat oval or round discs and deep-fried in vegetable oil until puffed and cooked through. Ham Chim Peng has a mildly salty, slightly sweet flavour with a soft, airy, and chewy texture inside and a lightly crisp crust. It is commonly eaten as a breakfast item or morning snack, often paired with congee (rice porridge) or consumed on its own. Some variants are filled with five-spice pork or red bean paste, but the plain salted version is the most common. Nutritionally, Ham Chim Peng is energy-dense at 339 kcal/100 g, with 53.8 g carbohydrate, 9.8 g fat, and 7.0 g protein per 100 g. Its dietary fibre content is low at 1.6 g/100 g and sodium is notable at 390 mg/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is high (~76), making it a high glycemic load food (GL ~32.8 per 80 g serving). Clinically, it is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high refined carbohydrate content, elevated sodium, deep-fried preparation, and pro-inflammatory AGE formation.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID: F11090020), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy fried dough analogue and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed source, which is the approved GI source. Multilingual names are well-established for this Cantonese dish.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.1 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for fried dough (item #18033, 'Bread, fried') as HPB Foodo does not report saturated fat breakdown for Ham Chim Peng. This is an approximate value and should be updated when HPB publishes detailed fatty acid data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Ham Chim Peng are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific Cantonese fried dough fritter as of 2026. 3. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on refined white-flour deep-fried dough products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for Ham Chim Peng exists in the approved GI database. 4. Glycemic load per serving = (76 × 43.04 g carb per 80 g serving) / 100 = 32.7, rounded to 32.8. 5. Halal status is assumed as the standard plain version contains no pork or alcohol; variants with pork filling would not be halal. 6. FoodOn match is approximate (FOODON_03315595, fried dough food product); no species-level FoodOn entry exists for Ham Chim Peng. 7. FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Doughnuts and similar fried dough products) is the closest defensible category match.
